report_name: Medicare Part C Reporting Requirements: Technical Specifications Document
metrics: Total number of grievances
Total number of timely notifications of grievance decisions
Number of expedited grievances
Number of dismissed grievances
Number of completed organization determinations (fully favorable, partially favorable, adverse)
Number of completed reconsiderations (fully favorable, partially favorable, adverse)
Number of withdrawn coverage requests
Number of dismissed coverage requests
Number of reopened organization determinations and reconsiderations
Employer Group Plan Sponsor enrollment count as of the last day of the reporting period
SNP Care Management: Number of eligible enrollees (Element A)
SNP Care Management: Number of enrollees eligible for an annual reassessment (Element B)
SNP Care Management: Number of initial HRAs completed (Element C)
SNP Care Management: Number of initial HRAs refused (Element D)
SNP Care Management: Number of initial HRAs where enrollee was unable to be reached (Element E)
SNP Care Management: Number of reassessments completed (Element F)
SNP Care Management: Number of reassessments refused (Element G)
SNP Care Management: Number of reassessments where enrollee was unable to be reached (Element H)
Total number of enrollment requests
Total number of enrollment requests requiring additional information
Total number of voluntary disenrollment transactions
Rewards and Incentives Programs: Number of currently enrolled members (Element G)
Rewards and Incentives Programs: Number of rewards made so far (Element H)
Payments to Providers: Total actual payments made to contracted providers (Element A)
Payments to Providers: Payments by value-based payment categories (Categories 1, 2, 3, and 4)
Supplemental Benefit Utilization and Costs: Unique count of eligible enrollees (Element H)
Supplemental Benefit Utilization and Costs: Number of enrollees utilizing benefit (Element I)
Supplemental Benefit Utilization and Costs: Units of utilization (Element G)
Supplemental Benefit Utilization and Costs: Total net amount incurred by the plan (Element L)
Supplemental Benefit Utilization and Costs: Total enrollee out-of-pocket costs (Element O)
D-SNP Enrollee Advisory Committee (EAC): Count of EAC meetings (Element B)
D-SNP Transmission of Admission Notifications: Count of hospital and SNF admissions (Element A)
D-SNP Transmission of Admission Notifications: Count of notifications to the state or state-designated entity (Element B)
dimensions: By Plan Benefit Package (PBP) Category Code
By Network Type (In-network, Out-of-network PPO, Out-of-network HMO-POS, Visitor/travel, Other)
By Offer Type (Mandatory, Optional, Uniformity Flexibility, SSBCI)
filters: In-network payments only (for Payments to Providers Elements B-S)
Beneficiary-initiated enrollment and disenrollment requests (excluding auto-assignments and CMS-initiated actions)
SNP Care Management Element A: Enrollees continually enrolled for at least 90 days, or fewer than 90 days if initial HRA completed
SNP Care Management Element B: Enrollees continuously enrolled for 365 days or more
Supplemental benefits approved and furnished within the contract year
business_rules: Grievances are reported based on the date the MAO provided the decision, regardless of when the request was received.
If an enrollee files a subsequent grievance on the same issue prior to the decision or deadline, report as one grievance; if filed after, report as separate grievances.
If an enrollee files a grievance about multiple issues, report as separate grievances.
Reopened cases must be reported in the quarter's file corresponding to when the beneficiary was notified of the reopening decision.
For Payments to Providers, if a provider is paid using multiple arrangements, report total dollars under the category representing the dominant payment arrangement.
Payments to Providers must be reported based on the calendar year the payment was made, regardless of when services were furnished.
SNP Care Management: The 365-day cycle period for the HRA begins the day after the date the previous HRA was completed.
SNP Care Management: To report an enrollee as 'unable to reach' (UTR), the plan must document at least 3 non-automated phone attempts and a follow-up letter.
Supplemental Benefits: If a plan offers a PBP category, it must submit data for that category even if no enrollees utilized the benefit (reporting zero for utilization and costs).
Supplemental Benefits: Debit cards are considered administrative tools, and plans must break down utilization and costs by specific service categories.
D-SNP EAC: If multiple D-SNPs share an EAC, one designated D-SNP reports the meeting details, and other participating D-SNPs reference the designated plan's response.
exclusions: Grievances filed by non-enrollees or prospective enrollees
Grievances or services related only to Medicaid benefits and not covered by Medicare
Dismissed grievances (excluded from the total grievance count, reported separately)
Claims payment or appeals from contract providers governed under contractual arrangements
Concurrent reviews during hospitalization, SNF, HHA, or CORF care that do not result in organization determinations
Duplicate payment requests and claims returned to a provider due to clerical error
Part B drugs paid or denied at the pharmacy point-of-sale (unless subsequently processed as an organization determination)
Enrollment/disenrollment requests for which a timely cancellation request is received
Voluntary disenrollments notified solely via Daily Transaction Reply Report (DTRR)
Payments for administrative services and payments to hospitals, facilities, pharmacies, or labs
Supplemental benefit costs and utilization for services that were denied
reporting_frequency: Varies by section (including Quarterly and Annual submissions)
notes: Data corrections and resubmissions must be submitted prior to March 31st (except for D-SNP Transmission of Admission Notifications, which must be submitted prior to May 31st).
MAOs must retain a complete archive of reporting data and documentation for a 10-year retention period.
Employer Group Plan Sponsor details (Tax ID, Address, Name) are proprietary and protected from public disclosure under FOIA.
All Part C Reporting Requirements inquiries should be sent to PartsCDPlanReportingAndDV@cms.hhs.gov.
Technical assistance for file formats and uploads is handled by the HPMS help desk (hpms@cms.hhs.gov).
